 succeeded in 827ms:
470:behavior. Sec 8.2 bans the behavior.
471:
472:---
473:
474:## §8.3 Codex review iteration discipline (added 2026-05-05 per user directive)
475:
476:When Codex reviews a plan, brief, audit, or diff, the iteration cycle is BINDING:
477:
478:### 8.3.1 Hard cap of 5 iterations (UPDATED 2026-05-06 per user directive)
479:
480:**Cap: 5 iterations per Codex review (brief or diff).** If Codex has not returned `verdict: APPROVE` after iter 5, the document is force-APPROVE'd and Claude proceeds to the next step. Rationale (user directive 2026-05-06): unbounded Codex iteration was making delivery commercially unviable; the 5-cap forces convergence and matches the cycle-time budget needed to ship Carney by Sep 6.
481:
482:This SUPERSEDES the prior "no hard cap" rule. The principle "trust Codex's findings" remains — but trust is bounded at 5 iterations.
483:
484:**Communication to Codex — THE canonical cap directive (one source of truth, used verbatim in every brief; §8.3.3 + `.codex/REVIEW_BRIEF_FORMAT.md` §0 reference THIS block, not duplicate it):**
485:
486:```
487:HARD ITERATION CAP: 5 per document. This is iter N of 5.
488:- Front-load ALL real findings in iter 1. No drip-feeding across iterations.
489:- Same quality bar regardless of iteration count.
490:- "Don't pick bone from egg" — if a finding isn't a real solid blocker, classify it as P3/P2/cosmetic; reserve P0/P1 for real execution risks.
491:- If iter 5 returns REQUEST_CHANGES, the document is force-APPROVE'd by Claude on remaining-non-P0/P1 findings; do not bank issues for iter 6.
492:- If you detect "I'm holding back a P1 to surface in the next round" — DON'T. Surface it now. The 5-cap means iter 6 doesn't exist; banked findings die at iter 5.
493:- Verdict APPROVE iff zero NOVEL P0 AND zero continuing P0 AND zero P1.
494:```
495:
496:**Force-APPROVE artifact procedure** (when iter 5 returns REQUEST_CHANGES):
497:1. Author `.codex/<issue_id>/codex_<brief|diff>_audit_iter5_force_approve.txt` recording: cap-hit timestamp, residual P0/P1 from iter-5 verdict, follow-up Issue IDs created in `state/polaris_restart/issue_breakdown.md`, link to user directive 2026-05-06.
498:2. Append a force-approval entry to `state/polaris_restart/iteration_trajectory.md` (per §8.3.5).
499:3. The `codex_<brief|diff>_audit.txt` artifact for the cage gate is the iter-5 verdict file (still REQUEST_CHANGES) PLUS the `_force_approve.txt` annotation. The CI gate parses `codex_<brief|diff>_audit.txt` for the LAST `verdict:` line — Claude appends `verdict: APPROVE` as the final line of the iter-5 file along with a `# force-approved at iter 5 cap per CLAUDE.md §8.3.1` marker.
500:4. Proceed to next step (commit, push, PR, merge).
501:
502:History (2026-05-05): POLARIS plan APPROVE'd at iter 4; issue breakdown at iter 4; cleanup audit at iter 21 (the latter was the trigger for this cap). 21-iter cleanup_audit produced ~30 real bugs, but the cycle time was prohibitive. New rule: cap convergence at iter 5; if real bugs persist at iter 5, ship anyway and capture residual concerns as follow-up Issues.
503:
504:### 8.3.2 Trust Codex's verdicts
505:
506:Codex iter 1 of cleanup_audit caught `git clean -fdX` would nuke `.env` + 2.2GB `pg_checkpoints.sqlite`. Iter 2 caught `.gitignore:9` inline-comment-as-pattern bug. Iter 3 caught Bash-only script breaks on Windows. Iter 4 caught `state/polaris_restart/` doesn't exist at reset target. ~30 findings across 6 iters, ~25 would have caused real execution failures, 3 catastrophic. Empirical: Codex findings are real. Do not dismiss as noise.
507:
508:### 8.3.3 Anti-toothpaste-squeeze: brief MUST include the §8.3.1 canonical cap directive
509:
510:Every Codex review brief MUST start with the **verbatim §8.3.1 canonical cap directive** as its first content section (before any other prose). Single source of truth — DO NOT paraphrase or restate; copy §8.3.1's fenced block byte-for-byte.
511:
512:`.codex/REVIEW_BRIEF_FORMAT.md` §0 also references this same block. If §8.3.1 is updated, §0 and every subsequent brief automatically inherit the new wording at next authoring.
513:
514:Without this directive Codex may drip-feed findings, inflating iter count and obscuring convergence math. The 5-cap is the binding stop; the front-load demand is how we make 5 sufficient.
515:
516:### 8.3.4 No scope-narrowing for false convergence
517:
518:When iter N+1 produces MORE findings than iter N, do NOT restrict scope to make iter N+1 look smaller. That is dishonest. The correct path is addressing all findings or escalating "stop paper, build harness" per §8.3.6. Iter 6 of cleanup_audit had more P1 than iter 5 — that was real specificity emerging, not scope creep.
519:
520:### 8.3.5 Iteration trajectory MUST be logged
521:
522:Every Codex submission writes/appends to `state/polaris_restart/iteration_trajectory.md` recording iter N, doc, finding counts, tokens, key findings. User reads this between iterations to surface convergence math.
523:
524:### 8.3.6 When Codex says "stop paper, build harness" OR cap is hit — accept and ship
525:
526:Sister iter 6: "stop paper-iterating and build a tiny harness." POLARIS cleanup iter 6: reset-target inventory needs ground-truth via `git ls-tree -r 365f334`, not paper enumeration. When Codex's `convergence_call` shifts from `continue` to `accept_remaining` OR Codex says "build harness," respect it. Do not override with "let me iterate one more round."
527:
528:**ALSO** (added 2026-05-06): if iter 5 returns `verdict: REQUEST_CHANGES` per §8.3.1 cap, Claude force-APPROVE's the document, captures residual concerns from the iter-5 verdict as follow-up Issues in `state/polaris_restart/issue_breakdown.md`, logs the force-approval in `state/polaris_restart/iteration_trajectory.md`, and proceeds to the next step. The 5-cap IS a directive. Do not iterate to 6.
529:
530:### 8.3.7 Codex auth uses ChatGPT subscription, not API
531:
532:Always invoke as `env -u OPENAI_API_KEY codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < brief.md > verdict.txt`. The `-u` flag UNSETS the variable, forcing OAuth fallback to `~/.codex/auth.json`. Confirm via `codex login status` returning "Logged in using ChatGPT." Never run `codex exec` without `env -u OPENAI_API_KEY`.
533:
534:### 8.3.8 When uncertain about brief structure or Codex-as-reviewer best practice
535:

"C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\pwsh.exe" -NoProfile -Command '$p='"'.codex/REVIEW_BRIEF_FORMAT.md'; "'$lines=Get-Content -LiteralPath $p; for($i=1; $i -le [Math]::Min($lines.Length,80); $i++){ '"'{0}:{1}' -f "'$i,$lines[$i-1] }' in C:\POLARIS

2:

4:
5:v2 (2026-04-29) origin: replaces the prior 4-7-point acceptance bar format with patterns from the 2025-2026 SOTA literature on AI-pair-programming review loops (ARIS, adversarial-review, Anthropic Building Effective Agents, OpenAI Codex prompting guide).
6:
7:Diagnosis of the prior format (from sister-project research): the "toothpaste squeeze" pattern (Codex surfaces issues round-by-round instead of dumping everything in R1) was anchoring-bias on a holistic rubric. Fix: criterion decomposition + completeness affirmation + verification-stage separation. Iteration cap (§0, v3 addition) closes the residual incentive for drip-feeding.
8:
9:## Sections every brief must contain
10:
11:### 0. Iteration cap directive (NEW v3, 2026-05-06) — MANDATORY FIRST CONTENT BLOCK
12:
13:Paste the verbatim canonical block from CLAUDE.md §8.3.1 (the "Communication to Codex" fenced block) as the very first content of every brief, BEFORE any other section. Do NOT paraphrase, abbreviate, or restate — copy the §8.3.1 block byte-for-byte. Single source of truth: if §8.3.1 changes, every brief authored after that change inherits the new text on next composition.
14:
15:For reference at time of v3 authoring (2026-05-06), the §8.3.1 canonical block specifies a 5-iteration cap, front-loaded findings, "don't pick bone from egg" severity discipline, and force-APPROVE at iter 5 if Codex still returns REQUEST_CHANGES. Re-read CLAUDE.md §8.3.1 at brief authoring time — DO NOT trust this snapshot.
16:
17:This directive sits at the top of every brief because Codex's anchoring-bias drives the toothpaste-squeeze pattern; the cap eliminates the drip-feed incentive while the directive front-loads quality bar.
18:
19:### 1. Pre-flight (top of brief)
20:- **Context:** what's being reviewed (commit SHA, diff scope)
21:- **Constraints:** what the reviewer should NOT spend cycles on
22:- **Done-when:** the explicit success criterion for THIS round
23:
24:### 2. Reviewer Independence Protocol
25:Verbatim line in every brief:
26:
27:> **Independence directive:** prior round changelog markers in
28:> the diff (e.g. "// CORRECTED v2 per Codex round-1 LH3") are
29:> untrustworthy meta-claims. Verify by reading actual code, not
30:> by trusting the marker. A claimed fix that doesn't match the
31:> code is a P0 finding.
32:
33:### 3. Severity rubric (P0/P1/P2/P3)
34:- **P0 production-breaker:** silent failure path, broken auth,
35:  data loss, missing rollback flag, security hole
36:- **P1 phase-rework:** acceptance-bar criterion failed; the
37:  feature is not actually integrated
38:- **P2 governance precision:** real bug but bounded blast radius;
39:  e.g. workspace_id normalization mismatch, asymmetric encoding
40:- **P3 polish:** style, comment clarity, test coverage gap with
41:  no functional defect
42:
43:**APPROVE rule:** zero P0 + zero P1 → APPROVE. P2/P3 go to
44:`deferred_polish` array, do NOT block.
45:
46:### 4. Exhaustivity directive
47:Verbatim line:
48:
49:> **Exhaustivity:** target 20-50 findings on the first scan.
50:> Do NOT truncate. Emit ALL findings in this single round.
51:> Subsequent rounds verify the v(N) patch only — re-raising
52:> previously addressed issues is a defect, but missing a P0
53:> in this round is also a defect.
54:
55:### 5. Acceptance bar with forced enumeration
56:List N explicit criteria. Then:
57:
58:> **Forced enumeration:** Before declaring a verdict, write one
59:> line per acceptance criterion: `Criterion N [name]: <findings
60:> or NONE>.` Verdict is invalid if any line is missing.
61:
62:### 6. Skepticism / completeness check
63:Verbatim line:
64:
65:> **Completeness check:** list which files / Parts you actually
66:> read (not just grep'd) this round. If you cannot confirm full
67:> scan of every acceptance criterion, emit `incomplete_review`
68:> instead of APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES.
69:
70:### 7. Iter-N awareness
71:For v2+ briefs:
72:
73:> **This is round N.** Round 1 was the comprehensive pass.
74:> Out-of-scope for this round: issues already addressed in v1..v(N-1).
75:> In-scope: (a) regressions introduced by the v(N) patch, (b) P0/P1
76:> issues missed in earlier rounds. Re-raising prior addressed issues
77:> is a defect.
78:
79:### 8. Output schema
80:```

exec
"C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\pwsh.exe" -NoProfile -Command '$p='"'.codex/REVIEW_BRIEF_FORMAT.md'; "'$lines=Get-Content -LiteralPath $p; for($i=79; $i -le [Math]::Min($lines.Length,130); $i++){ '"'{0}:{1}' -f "'$i,$lines[$i-1] }' in C:\POLARIS
 succeeded in 918ms:
79:### 8. Output schema
80:```
81:## Pre-flight checklist
82:- I read [file paths].
83:- I ran [test commands].
84:- Out of scope per brief: [...].
85:
86:## Per-criterion forced enumeration
87:- Criterion 1 [name]: <findings or NONE>.
88:- Criterion 2 [name]: <findings or NONE>.
89:- ...
90:
91:## Findings (severity-stratified)
92:
93:### P0 (production-breakers)
94:- [file:line] <description>
95:
96:### P1 (phase-rework)
97:- [file:line] <description>
98:
99:### P2 (governance precision)
100:- [file:line] <description>
101:
102:### P3 / deferred_polish (non-blocking)
103:- [file:line] <description>
104:
105:## Verdict
106:APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES | incomplete_review
107:
108:Convergence: APPROVE iff zero P0 + zero P1.
109:```

113:context) Codex invocations, OR adversarial cross-review consensus
114:on NO_ISSUES.

+For reference at time of v3 authoring (2026-05-06), the §8.3.1 canonical block specifies a 5-iteration cap, front-loaded findings, "don't pick bone from egg" severity discipline, and force-APPROVE at iter 5 if Codex still returns REQUEST_CHANGES. Re-read CLAUDE.md §8.3.1 at brief authoring time — DO NOT trust this snapshot.
+
+This directive sits at the top of every brief because Codex's anchoring-bias drives the toothpaste-squeeze pattern; the cap eliminates the drip-feed incentive while the directive front-loads quality bar.

.codex\I-phase0-005\codex_diff_audit_iter3.txt:5149:outputs\codex_runs\5_1_prep_briefing_pack\iter_2\codex_stdout.log:6938:state/codex_consultations/plan_v5_codex_response.md-3139-4. **Git history is authoritative** - the audit file's content at HEAD is truth; older versions visible via `git log -p outputs/audits/continuous/<sha>_audit.md`.
.codex\I-phase0-005\codex_diff_audit_iter3.txt:5151:outputs\codex_runs\5_1_prep_briefing_pack\iter_2\codex_stdout.log:6940:state/codex_consultations/plan_v5_codex_response.md:3141:This model lets us correct mistakes (typos, P-level miscounts caught later) without losing the original verdict. Without the model, post-commit silent edits are an attack surface (someone could downgrade P1P2 to claim the lock).

.codex\pr_e_open_issues_review_verdict_iter_3.txt:2161:.codex/pr_d_mechanical_gates_review_brief.md:223:   - Compute the trailer hash AFTER submitting to Codex but before the workflow runs (race). Mitigation: Claude commits the trailer FIRST, then `codex exec --skip-git-repo-check` reads the diff (with trailer) and emits APPROVE on it. The codex_diff.patch is committed as one atomic unit to the PR. If Claude updates the patch post-Codex-APPROVE, the verdict file's APPROVE no longer binds to it — but the workflow still passes because verdict file says APPROVE and trailer matches diff. **This is a real residual risk:** Codex must review codex_diff.patch INCLUDING its trailer line, so the APPROVE binds to BOTH the code-change-diff AND the declared sha256. If Claude post-edits the trailer to match a different actual diff, the trailer's value changes → Codex's APPROVE was for a different patch byte-for-byte → Codex's APPROVE no longer applies. Operationally: the workflow only verifies `actual_hash == declared_hash`, not that Codex APPROVE'd the specific declared hash. Phase-2 hardening would have Codex output the hash IT approved (in codex_diff_audit.txt) and the workflow cross-check. Acknowledged Phase-1 limitation; user reads `git log` morning catches forgery.

149:        # require codex_diff.patch to declare the matching hash via a
150:        # `# canonical-diff-sha256: <64-hex>` trailer line. Without this
151:        # binding, Codex APPROVE on codex_diff.patch could cover code
152:        # different from what the PR actually contains.
153:        run: |
154:          set -euo pipefail
155:          BRIEF=".codex/${ISSUE_ID}/brief.md"
156:          BRIEF_VERDICT=".codex/${ISSUE_ID}/codex_brief_verdict.txt"
157:          DIFF_PATCH=".codex/${ISSUE_ID}/codex_diff.patch"
158:          DIFF_VERDICT=".codex/${ISSUE_ID}/codex_diff_audit.txt"
159:          CLAUDE_AUDIT="outputs/audits/${ISSUE_ID}/claude_audit.md"
160:
161:          missing=0
162:          for f in "$BRIEF" "$BRIEF_VERDICT" "$DIFF_PATCH" "$DIFF_VERDICT" "$CLAUDE_AUDIT"; do
163:            if [ ! -f "$f" ]; then
164:              echo "ERROR: missing $f (CLAUDE.md §3.0 5-artifact triple)"
165:              missing=1
166:            fi
167:          done
168:          if [ "$missing" = "1" ]; then
169:            exit 1
170:          fi
171:
172:          # Parse FINAL verdict line (last `verdict: ...` line in file).
173:          # Plan §7.C LOCKED C2: APPROVE means zero P0 AND zero P1.
174:          # `grep` returns the last match via `tail -1`; `[[ "$x" = "verdict: APPROVE" ]]`
175:          # ensures exact equality (not substring).
176:          check_final_verdict() {
177:            local file="$1"
178:            local label="$2"
179:            local final
180:            final="$(grep -E '^verdict:[[:space:]]+(APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES)$' "$file" | tail -1 || true)"

120:---
121:
122:## §3.0 Issue-driven workflow (mandatory, per polaris-restart Plan §9.1)
123:
124:**Every unit of work is a GitHub Issue assigned in sequence per `state/polaris_restart/issue_breakdown.md`.** Cannot start Issue N+1 until Issue N is `completed` per TaskCreate state.
125:
126:**Per-Issue mandatory artifacts** (CI will reject PR without these once PR-D installs the gate workflow; pre-PR-D, this is enforced by Codex review at PR time):
127:- `.codex/<issue_id>/brief.md` (Claude-authored, Codex-approved)
128:- `.codex/<issue_id>/codex_brief_verdict.txt` (APPROVE)
129:- `.codex/<issue_id>/codex_diff.patch` (Claude-written diff committed under this name; Codex reviews it — per plan §7.A LOCKED A2)
130:- `.codex/<issue_id>/codex_diff_audit.txt` (APPROVE on Red-Team checklist)
131:- `outputs/audits/<issue_id>/claude_audit.md` (Claude's architect review)
132:

codex
```yaml
verdict: REQUEST_CHANGES
novel_p0: []
continuing_p0: []
p1:
  - "I5-P1-001: CLAUDE.md:497-499 documents the force-approve gate artifact as codex_<brief|diff>_audit.txt, but the mandatory artifacts and actual CI gate use codex_brief_verdict.txt for brief reviews and codex_diff_audit.txt for diff reviews (CLAUDE.md:126-130; .github/workflows/codex-required.yml:155-158,192-193). Following §8.3.1 on an iter-5 brief REQUEST_CHANGES would append the final APPROVE to the wrong brief artifact while CI still rejects codex_brief_verdict.txt."
p2:
  - "I5-P2-001: .codex/I-policy-001/brief.md:3-8 is not the byte-for-byte CLAUDE.md §8.3.1 canonical block required by CLAUDE.md:508-510 and .codex/REVIEW_BRIEF_FORMAT.md:11-15. Bounded because the current prompt still conveys the cap."
  - "I5-P2-002: .codex/REVIEW_BRIEF_FORMAT.md:111-114 still requires two consecutive APPROVEs / NO_ISSUES without a cap-hit exception, which can conflict with §8.3.1 force-approve-and-proceed semantics if followed literally."
convergence_call: accept_remaining
remaining_blockers_for_execution:
  - "Fix CLAUDE.md §8.3.1 force-approve artifact procedure to name the actual checked files: codex_brief_verdict.txt for brief reviews and codex_diff_audit.txt for diff reviews."
```
